text.title: IaC PR Review Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable axis rules every IaC PR review must apply. A PR may not be approved if any axis returns a block verdict.

rule.statement: Map every changed resource to a blast radius tier (S0 = global outage if misconfigured, S1 = single-region outage, S2 = single service, S3 = sandbox). Reject PR if blast radius tier is unstated for ANY changed resource, OR if S0 / S1 changes lack a documented mitigation (canary, feature flag, or maintenance window).
rule.rationale: Most production IaC outages stem from a high-blast-radius change reviewed without the reviewer realizing the tier. Forcing per-resource tier surfaces the risk upfront.

rule.statement: Every IAM-policy / role-binding / service-account change requires an explicit reviewer comment naming: principal, added_permissions, removed_permissions, scope_of_resources. Reject PR if IAM changes are present without this summary, OR if any added_permission grants wildcard ("*") action on production resources.
rule.rationale: Silent IAM escalation is the most common audit finding. Wildcard policies appear regularly because the original author "just needed it to work."

rule.statement: For Terraform / OpenTofu PRs: reviewer must verify the plan output contains no destroy-and-recreate operations on stateful resources (databases, queues, storage buckets, persistent volumes). Reject PR if such operations are detected, unless a documented state-migration ticket is linked.
rule.rationale: Destroy-and-recreate on a stateful resource silently drops production data. Plan-output review catches this in seconds.

rule.statement: Verify: (a) no plaintext secrets in diff, (b) secrets-manager references resolve via supported syntax, (c) any new secret has a documented rotation policy, (d) GHA workflows referencing `secrets.X` declare `permissions:` block. Reject PR on any failure.
rule.rationale: Even encrypted-at-rest secrets leak through unscoped GHA permissions or untemplated secret references. The four sub-checks are non-optional.

rule.statement: PR description must include a rollback plan with: rollback_command, expected_state_after_rollback, max_time_to_rollback. Reject PR if any of these three fields is missing, vague ("revert the commit"), or unverified.
rule.rationale: Outages where rollback is undefined extend MTTR by 3-10×. Pre-defined rollback turns recovery into a deterministic action.

example.title: Reviewer comment with per-axis verdict
example.code:
## Review
[blast_radius] S1 — RDS instance + IAM role. Mitigation: maintenance window declared 2026-05-22 23:00 UTC. ✅ [iam] added: rds:Describe* on prod-db (scoped). removed: none. no wildcards. ✅ [state_safety] plan output: 0 destroy + 0 recreate. ✅ [secrets] no plaintext secrets. RDS password sourced from aws_secretsmanager_secret_version.db_pwd. rotation = 90d. ✅ [rollback] rollback_command: `terraform apply -target=... -auto-approve` expected_state: prior tag v3.4.1. max_time: 8 min. ✅
Verdict: APPROVE.
example.note: Illustrates the five axes producing a single approval verdict.

example.title: Rubber-stamp review
example.note: Illustrates the absence of every axis. PRs approved with "LGTM" alone fail this methodology.
